zh.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser是Sir Hugh le Despenser和Mary de Quincy的兒子，於1170年出生在英國盧特藍德。他於1185年與Rohese de Foix Recuara在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾德福德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1218年之前在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾阿內斯比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser是Sir Hugh le Despenser和Mary de Quincy的儿子，于1170年出生在英国盧特藍德。他于1185年与Rohese de Foix Recuara在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾德福德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1218年之前在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾阿內斯比。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser was born in 1170, in Rutland, England as the son of Sir Hugh le Despenser and Mary de Quincy. He married Rohese de Foix Recuara in 1185, in Defford, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died before 1218, in Arnesby, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser est né en 1170, à Rutland, Angleterre, fils de Sir Hugh le Despenser et Mary de Quincy. Il a épousé Rohese de Foix Recuara en 1185, à Defford, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 1218, à Arnesby, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser wurde 1170 in Rutland, England, als Sohn von Sir Hugh le Despenser und Mary de Quincy geboren. Er hat 1185 in Defford, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom Rohese de Foix Recuara geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 1218 in Arnesby, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser è nato il nel 1170 nel Rutland, England come figlio di Sir Hugh le Despenser e Mary de Quincy. Lui sposato Rohese de Foix Recuara nel 1185, nel Defford, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 1218, nel Arnesby, Leicestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenserは1170年にイギリスルトランドでSir Hugh le DespenserとMary de Quincyの息子として生まれた。1185年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアデフォードでRohese de Foix Recuaraと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1218年以前にイギリスイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアアルネスビーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser(은)는 1170년 영국 루틀랜드에서 Sir Hugh le Despenser(와)과 Mary de Quincy의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1185년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 데퍼드에서 Rohese de Foix Recuara(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1218년 이전 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 아네스비에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser nasceu em 1170, em Rutland, Inglaterra filho de Sir Hugh le Despenser e Mary de Quincy. Ele casou-se com Rohese de Foix Recuara em 1185, em Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 1218, em Arnesby, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser родился в 1170 г. в местности рутланд, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir Hugh le Despenser и Mary de Quincy. Он женился на женщине по имени Rohese de Foix Recuara в 1185 г. в местности деффорд, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 1218 г. в местности арнесби, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas le Despenser nació en 1170, en Rutland, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Hugh le Despenser y Mary de Quincy. Se casó con Rohese de Foix Recuara en 1185, en Defford, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 1218, en Arnesby, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
